---
title: "child_bearing_replication"
author: "Alexander Unger"
date: "2026-05-29"
output: html_document
---
```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# Angrist & Evans (1998): Childbearing and Labor Supply
# SUW Table replication — kappa estimators, blockwise procedure
#
# Design:
#   Z = samesex  (1 if first two children same sex — near-random instrument)
#   D = morekids (1 if mother had a third child)
#   Y = workedm  (LFP, binary) and lincomem (log income)
#
# Near one-sided noncompliance: almost no always-takers (a mother who
# would have a third child regardless of child sexes is very rare).
# This makes the Abadie kappa near Proposition 3.3 territory and creates
# the most dramatic translation-invariance failure in the thesis.
#

 
# ==============================================================================
# §0  PACKAGES
# ==============================================================================
# haven     : read Stata .dta files
# AER       : ivreg() for 2SLS benchmark
# sandwich  : HC1 robust variance for 2SLS
# lmtest    : coeftest() for robust 2SLS standard errors
 
library(haven)
library(AER)
library(sandwich)
library(lmtest)
library(knitr)

# ==============================================================================
# §1  DATA LOADING AND PREPARATION
# ==============================================================================
# The replication uses ae98.dta from the SUW replication package.
# Set data_path to the folder containing ae98.dta.
#
# Key variable definitions (Angrist & Evans 1998 Census data):
#   workedm    : 1 if mother worked in year prior to Census (LFP outcome)
#   incomem    : mother's annual income (in dollars)
#   morekids   : 1 if mother had three or more children (endogenous treatment)
#   samesex    : 1 if first two children are the same sex (instrument)
#   agem       : mother's age
#   agefstm    : mother's age at first birth
#   sexk       : sex of first child (1 = boy)
#   sex2nd     : sex of second child (1 = boy)
#   blackm     : 1 if Black
#   hispm      : 1 if Hispanic
#   othracem   : 1 if other race
#
# Translation-invariance test (mirrors SUW Stata script lines):
#   Stata creates: workedm_21 = workedm + 1   (shift by +1)
#                  workedm_01 = -(workedm - 1) = 1 - workedm   (recentred)
#                  lincomem_cnt = ln(100 * incomem)  (shift by ln(100))
#                  lincomem_ths = ln(incomem / 1000) (shift by -ln(1000))
#                  lincomem_hths= ln(incomem / 100000)
#   These shifts are the cents/dollars analog for the AE design.
#   Translation-invariant estimators (tau_u, tau_a10) must give identical
#   estimates across all income scales; unnormalized estimators (tau_a, etc.)
#   will diverge dramatically — the most extreme failure in the thesis.
# ==============================================================================
 
data_path <- "/Users/alexung/Desktop/master_thesis/code/"   # <--- USER: set this path
ae98 <- read_dta(file.path(data_path, "ae98.dta"))
 
# --- Sample restriction ---
# Use the full sample as distributed (Stata do-file drops a pre-existing
# lincomem variable and recreates it, so we replicate that here).
 
ae98 <- ae98[!is.na(ae98$workedm) & !is.na(ae98$morekids) & !is.na(ae98$samesex), ]
 
# --- Outcome variables ---
# LFP (binary)
ae98$workedm_21  <- ae98$workedm + 1         # shift +1 (translation test)
ae98$workedm_01  <- 1 - ae98$workedm         # re-centring (translation test)
 
# Log income — drop and recreate to match Stata do-file exactly
# The raw incomem variable is in dollars; income = 0 causes log issues.
# Following SUW: keep observations where incomem > 0 for income regressions
# (LFP regressions use the full sample).
ae98$lincomem      <- log(ae98$incomem)
ae98$lincomem_cnt  <- log(100  * ae98$incomem)   # shift by +ln(100)
ae98$lincomem_ths  <- log(ae98$incomem / 1000)   # shift by -ln(1000)
ae98$lincomem_hths <- log(ae98$incomem / 100000) # shift by -ln(100000)
 
# Subsets: full sample for LFP, positive-income sample for income regressions
ae98_lfp    <- ae98                               # N ~ 254,654
ae98_income <- ae98[ae98$incomem > 0, ]           # N ~ subset with positive income
 
cat("N (LFP sample):    ", nrow(ae98_lfp), "\n")
cat("N (income sample): ", nrow(ae98_income), "\n")


make_X_ae <- function(df) {
  X <- model.matrix(~ agem + agefstm + sexk + sex2nd + blackm + hispm + othracem,
                    data = df)
  # model.matrix includes intercept by default (column "(Intercept)" = 1)
  X
}
 
X_lfp    <- make_X_ae(ae98_lfp)
X_income <- make_X_ae(ae98_income)
 
cat("Covariate matrix dimensions (LFP):    ", dim(X_lfp), "\n")
cat("Covariate matrix dimensions (income): ", dim(X_income), "\n")

 
```
 
```{r}
 # ==============================================================================
# §3  FUNCTIONS
# ==============================================================================
# Source the shared function file — identical to Card replication.
# Functions used:
#   safe_logit()              : clipped logistic (§3a)
#   prep_design_for_mest()    : standardise X for M-estimation (§3b)
#   logit_mle()               : MLE propensity score (§3c)
#   fit_logit_alpha()         : MLE for sandwich (§3d)
#   fit_cbps_alpha()          : CBPS propensity score (§3e)
#   cbps() / get_cbps_p()     : CBPS wrappers (§3f)
#   kappa_weights()           : kappa, kappa1, kappa0 (§3g)
#   tau_u()                   : Uysal normalized (§3h)
#   tau_a10()                 : Abadie-Cattaneo normalized (§3i)
#   tau_unnorm()              : unnormalized estimators (§3j)
#   kappa_outcome_weights()   : omega vectors (§3k)
#   num_jacobian()            : numerical Jacobian (§3l)
#   matrix_inverse_safe()     : robust matrix inverse (§3m)
#   sandwich_se_mest()        : sandwich SE from stacked moments (§3n)
#   alpha_moment_matrix()     : propensity score moment matrix (§3o)
#   kappa_analytic_se_one()   : analytical SE for one estimator (§3p)
#   safe_kappa_se()           : error-safe SE wrapper (§3q)
#   kappa_analytic_se_all()   : all six estimates + SEs (§3r)
#   run_2sls()                : 2SLS with HC1-robust SEs (§3s)
#   weight_diag()             : weight diagnostics (§3t)
#   check_weight_identity()   : algebraic check sum(w*Y) = tau (§3u)
#   fmt()                     : output formatter with stars (§3v)
# ==============================================================================
 
functions_path <- "/Users/alexung/Desktop/master_thesis/showing/functions_card.R"  # <--- USER: set this path
source(functions_path)

```


```{r}
 
# ==============================================================================
# §4  PROPENSITY SCORE ESTIMATION
# ==============================================================================
# Estimate p(Z=1|X) = P(samesex=1|X) once per sample.
# CBPS is used for tau_cb_u; MLE for all other estimators.
# Both methods use the same design matrix.
# ==============================================================================
 
cat("\n--- Estimating propensity scores ---\n")
 
# LFP sample
p_ml_lfp <- logit_mle(ae98_lfp$samesex, X_lfp)
cat("LFP p_ml: range [", round(min(p_ml_lfp),4), ",", round(max(p_ml_lfp),4), "]\n")
 
p_cb_lfp <- get_cbps_p(ae98_lfp$samesex, X_lfp)
cat("LFP p_cb: range [", round(min(p_cb_lfp),4), ",", round(max(p_cb_lfp),4), "]\n")
 
# Income sample
p_ml_income <- logit_mle(ae98_income$samesex, X_income)
cat("Income p_ml: range [", round(min(p_ml_income),4), ",", round(max(p_ml_income),4), "]\n")
 
p_cb_income <- get_cbps_p(ae98_income$samesex, X_income)
cat("Income p_cb: range [", round(min(p_cb_income),4), ",", round(max(p_cb_income),4), "]\n")
 
# Propensity score summary: samesex is near-random (~50%), so p should
# be very close to 0.5 for all observations — confirming near-random assignment.
# This is the AE analog of the draft lottery: design dominates learner.
 
```


```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# §5  POINT ESTIMATES + STANDARD ERRORS
# ==============================================================================
# Helper: one-shot call for a given (Y, Z, D, X, p_ml, p_cb) combination.
# Returns a named list: estimates (length 6) + se (length 6).
# Wraps kappa_analytic_se_all() and adds 2SLS.
# ==============================================================================
 
run_block <- function(Y, Z, D, X, label = "") {
  cat("\n--- Running block:", label, " (n =", length(Y), ") ---\n")
  res <- kappa_analytic_se_all(Y, Z, D, X)
  # 2SLS benchmark
  X_df <- as.data.frame(X[, colnames(X) != "(Intercept)", drop = FALSE])
  tsls <- run_2sls(Y, D, Z, X_df, endog_name = "D")
  res$tsls_coef <- tsls$coef
  res$tsls_se   <- tsls$se
  res
}
 
# --- Block 1: workedm (LFP, original scale) ---
blk_lfp_01 <- run_block(
  Y = ae98_lfp$workedm,
  Z = ae98_lfp$samesex,
  D = ae98_lfp$morekids,
  X = X_lfp,
  label = "workedm (original)"
)
 
# --- Block 2: workedm + 1 (shift +1, translation test) ---
blk_lfp_21 <- run_block(
  Y = ae98_lfp$workedm_21,
  Z = ae98_lfp$samesex,
  D = ae98_lfp$morekids,
  X = X_lfp,
  label = "workedm + 1"
)
 
# --- Block 3: 1 - workedm (re-centred, translation test) ---
blk_lfp_neg <- run_block(
  Y = ae98_lfp$workedm_01,
  Z = ae98_lfp$samesex,
  D = ae98_lfp$morekids,
  X = X_lfp,
  label = "1 - workedm"
)
 
# --- Block 4: lincomem (log income, dollars) ---
blk_inc_dollars <- run_block(
  Y = ae98_income$lincomem,
  Z = ae98_income$samesex,
  D = ae98_income$morekids,
  X = X_income,
  label = "lincomem (dollars)"
)
 
# --- Block 5: lincomem_cnt (log income, cents = dollars * 100) ---
blk_inc_cents <- run_block(
  Y = ae98_income$lincomem_cnt,
  Z = ae98_income$samesex,
  D = ae98_income$morekids,
  X = X_income,
  label = "lincomem_cnt (cents)"
)
 
# --- Block 6: lincomem_ths (log income, thousands) ---
blk_inc_ths <- run_block(
  Y = ae98_income$lincomem_ths,
  Z = ae98_income$samesex,
  D = ae98_income$morekids,
  X = X_income,
  label = "lincomem_ths (thousands)"
)
 
# --- Block 7: lincomem_hths (log income, hundred-thousands) ---
blk_inc_hths <- run_block(
  Y = ae98_income$lincomem_hths,
  Z = ae98_income$samesex,
  D = ae98_income$morekids,
  X = X_income,
  label = "lincomem_hths (hundred-thousands)"
)
```

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# §6  CLEAN MAIN RESULTS TABLES
# ==============================================================================
# Produces two readable HTML tables:
#   Table 6a: Labor force participation
#   Table 6b: Log income
#
# Important:
#   - For "1 if did not work, 0 otherwise", SUW report the additive inverse.
#     Therefore we multiply that column by -1 for display.
#   - Income columns are ordered as in SUW Table 4:
#       cents, dollars, $1000s, $100,000s.
# ==============================================================================

# small helper function jst wtih knitting purpose and run by run purpose

make_result_table <- function(blocks, col_labels, signs, caption) {
  out_html <- est_rows[, c("Panel", "Estimator")]
  out_plain <- est_rows[, c("Panel", "Estimator")]

  for (j in seq_along(blocks)) {
    out_html[[col_labels[j]]] <- vapply(
      est_rows$est_key,
      function(e) get_cell(blocks[[j]], e, sign = signs[j]),
      character(1)
    )

    out_plain[[gsub("<br>|<em>|</em>", " ", col_labels[j])]] <- vapply(
      est_rows$est_key,
      function(e) {
        if (e == "tsls") {
          est_val <- signs[j] * as.numeric(blocks[[j]]$tsls_coef)
          se_val  <- as.numeric(blocks[[j]]$tsls_se)
        } else {
          est_val <- signs[j] * as.numeric(blocks[[j]]$estimates[e])
          se_val  <- as.numeric(blocks[[j]]$se[e])
        }

        sprintf("%.3f (%0.3f)", est_val, se_val)
      },
      character(1)
    )
  }

  if (isTRUE(getOption("knitr.in.progress"))) {
    knitr::kable(
      out_html,
      format = "html",
      escape = FALSE,
      row.names = FALSE,
      caption = caption,
      align = c("l", "l", rep("c", length(blocks)))
    )
  } else {
    cat("\n", caption, "\n", sep = "")
    print(out_plain)
    invisible(out_plain)
  }
}



stars_from_est <- function(est, se) {
  if (is.na(est) || is.na(se) || se <= 0) return("")
  pval <- 2 * (1 - pnorm(abs(est / se)))
  if (pval < 0.01) return("***")
  if (pval < 0.05) return("**")
  if (pval < 0.10) return("*")
  ""
}

fmt_html <- function(est, se, sign = 1) {
  est2 <- sign * as.numeric(est)
  se2  <- as.numeric(se)
  paste0(
    sprintf("%.3f%s", est2, stars_from_est(est2, se2)),
    "<br><span style='font-size:90%'>( ",
    sprintf("%.3f", se2),
    " )</span>"
  )
}

get_cell <- function(blk, est, sign = 1) {
  if (est == "tsls") {
    return(fmt_html(blk$tsls_coef, blk$tsls_se, sign = sign))
  }

  fmt_html(blk$estimates[est], blk$se[est], sign = sign)
}

est_rows <- data.frame(
  Panel = c(
    "A. 2SLS",
    "B. Normalized estimates",
    "B. Normalized estimates",
    "B. Normalized estimates",
    "C. Unnormalized estimates",
    "C. Unnormalized estimates",
    "C. Unnormalized estimates"
  ),
  Estimator = c(
    "2SLS",
    "tau_cb_u",
    "tau_ml_u",
    "tau_ml_a10",
    "tau_ml_a",
    "tau_ml_t = tau_a1",
    "tau_ml_a0"
  ),
  est_key = c(
    "tsls",
    "tau_cb_u",
    "tau_ml_u",
    "tau_ml_a10",
    "tau_ml_a",
    "tau_ml_t",
    "tau_ml_a0"
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)



# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Table 6a: Labor force participation
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Column 3 is sign-flipped because SUW report the additive inverse
# for the "1 if did not work, 0 otherwise" coding.

lfp_blocks <- list(
  blk_lfp_01,
  blk_lfp_21,
  blk_lfp_neg
)

lfp_labels <- c(
  "1 if worked,<br>0 otherwise",
  "2 if worked,<br>1 otherwise",
  "1 if did not work,<br>0 otherwise<br><em>reported as additive inverse</em>"
)

lfp_signs <- c(1, 1, -1)

make_result_table(
  blocks = lfp_blocks,
  col_labels = lfp_labels,
  signs = lfp_signs,
  caption = "Angrist & Evans childbearing design: labor force participation"
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Table 6b: Log income
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Ordered exactly as SUW Table 4:
#   cents, dollars, $1000s, $100,000s.

income_blocks <- list(
  blk_inc_cents,
  blk_inc_dollars,
  blk_inc_ths,
  blk_inc_hths
)

income_labels <- c(
  "Cents",
  "Dollars",
  "$1000s",
  "$100,000s"
)

income_signs <- c(1, 1, 1, 1)

make_result_table(
  blocks = income_blocks,
  col_labels = income_labels,
  signs = income_signs,
  caption = "Angrist & Evans childbearing design: log income"
)
```


```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# §7  TRANSLATION-INVARIANCE VERIFICATION
# ==============================================================================
# For LFP, the third column is sign-flipped because SUW report the additive
# inverse of the estimate for 1 - workedm.
# ==============================================================================

estimator_names <- c(
  "tau_cb_u",
  "tau_ml_u",
  "tau_ml_a10",
  "tau_ml_a",
  "tau_ml_t",
  "tau_ml_a0"
)

ti_check_df <- function(blocks, signs, label_group) {
  rows <- lapply(estimator_names, function(est) {
    vals_raw <- sapply(blocks, function(blk) as.numeric(blk$estimates[est]))
    vals     <- vals_raw * signs

    rng <- max(vals, na.rm = TRUE) - min(vals, na.rm = TRUE)

    status <- if (est %in% c("tau_cb_u", "tau_ml_u", "tau_ml_a10")) {
      if (abs(rng) < 1e-4) "PASS"
      else "FAIL"
    } else {
      "Expected to vary"
    }

    data.frame(
      Outcome_group = label_group,
      Estimator = est,
      Min = round(min(vals, na.rm = TRUE), 6),
      Max = round(max(vals, na.rm = TRUE), 6),
      Range = round(rng, 6),
      Status = status,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  })

  do.call(rbind, rows)
}

ti_lfp <- ti_check_df(
  blocks = lfp_blocks,
  signs = lfp_signs,
  label_group = "Labor force participation"
)

ti_income <- ti_check_df(
  blocks = income_blocks,
  signs = income_signs,
  label_group = "Log income"
)

ti_results <- rbind(ti_lfp, ti_income)

knitr::kable(
  ti_results,
  format = "html",
  row.names = FALSE,
  caption = "Translation-invariance check"
)

```
 
 
 
 
```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# §8  WEIGHT DIAGNOSTICS
# ==============================================================================
# Compute outcome weights for the primary outcome (workedm, lincomem) and
# report the standard diagnostics: Sum_w, ESS, % negative, Max |w|.
#
# Key expected findings (thesis Section 5.2):
#   - Near-random samesex instrument → p ≈ 0.5 everywhere
#   - ESS similar across estimators (design dominates learner, as in Vietnam)
#   - Near one-sided noncompliance → kappa0 weights near zero → ESS very large
#     for tau_a0 (denominator ≈ 0 risk)
# ==============================================================================
 
cat("\n")
cat(paste(rep("-", 60), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("WEIGHT DIAGNOSTICS\n")
cat(paste(rep("-", 60), collapse = ""), "\n")
 
compute_weight_diags <- function(Y, Z, D, p_ml, p_cb, label) {
  cat("\n", label, "\n", sep = "")
  ow_ml <- kappa_outcome_weights(Z, D, p_ml)
  ow_cb <- kappa_outcome_weights(Z, D, p_cb)
 
  diag_list <- list(
    weight_diag(ow_cb$w_u,   "tau_cb_u  (CBPS)"),
    weight_diag(ow_ml$w_u,   "tau_ml_u  (MLE)"),
    weight_diag(ow_ml$w_a10, "tau_ml_a10"),
    weight_diag(ow_ml$w_a,   "tau_ml_a"),
    weight_diag(ow_ml$w_a1,  "tau_ml_t"),
    weight_diag(ow_ml$w_a0,  "tau_ml_a0")
  )
  diag_df <- do.call(rbind, diag_list)
  print(diag_df, row.names = FALSE)
 
  # Algebraic check: sum(w * Y) = tau_hat (should hold to machine precision)
  cat("\n  Algebraic check  sum(w * Y) == tau_hat:\n")
  checks <- c(
    tau_cb_u   = check_weight_identity(ow_cb$w_u,   Y, tau_u(Y, Z, D, p_cb)),
    tau_ml_u   = check_weight_identity(ow_ml$w_u,   Y, tau_u(Y, Z, D, p_ml)),
    tau_ml_a10 = check_weight_identity(ow_ml$w_a10, Y, tau_a10(Y, Z, D, p_ml)),
    tau_ml_a   = check_weight_identity(ow_ml$w_a,   Y, tau_unnorm(Y, Z, D, p_ml, "a")),
    tau_ml_t   = check_weight_identity(ow_ml$w_a1,  Y, tau_unnorm(Y, Z, D, p_ml, "a1")),
    tau_ml_a0  = check_weight_identity(ow_ml$w_a0,  Y, tau_unnorm(Y, Z, D, p_ml, "a0"))
  )
  print(checks)
}
 
compute_weight_diags(
  Y     = ae98_lfp$workedm,
  Z     = ae98_lfp$samesex,
  D     = ae98_lfp$morekids,
  p_ml  = p_ml_lfp,
  p_cb  = p_cb_lfp,
  label = "LFP outcome (workedm)"
)
 
compute_weight_diags(
  Y     = ae98_income$lincomem,
  Z     = ae98_income$samesex,
  D     = ae98_income$morekids,
  p_ml  = p_ml_income,
  p_cb  = p_cb_income,
  label = "Income outcome (lincomem, dollars)"
)

```



```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# §9  COMPLIER SHARE AND PROPENSITY SCORE SUMMARY
# ==============================================================================
# The complier share = E[kappa] = E[D(1) > D(0)] is estimated as mean(kappa).
# Under near one-sided noncompliance:
#   mean(kappa)  ≈ mean(kappa1)  (almost no always-takers, so kappa ≈ kappa1)
#   mean(kappa0) ≈ 0             (almost no untreated compliers in Z=0 group)
#
# This explains why tau_a0 has a near-zero denominator and explodes under
# translation shifts — it is dividing by the near-zero always-taker share.
# ==============================================================================
 
cat("\n")
cat(paste(rep("-", 60), collapse = ""), "\n")
cat("COMPLIER SHARE AND PROPENSITY SCORE SUMMARY\n")
cat(paste(rep("-", 60), collapse = ""), "\n")
 
kw_lfp    <- kappa_weights(ae98_lfp$samesex, ae98_lfp$morekids, p_ml_lfp)
kw_income <- kappa_weights(ae98_income$samesex, ae98_income$morekids, p_ml_income)
 
cat("\nLFP sample:\n")
cat(sprintf("  mean(kappa)  = %8.4f  [complier share]\n", mean(kw_lfp$kappa)))
cat(sprintf("  mean(kappa1) = %8.4f  [should ≈ kappa under one-sided NC]\n", mean(kw_lfp$kappa1)))
cat(sprintf("  mean(kappa0) = %8.4f  [should ≈ 0 under one-sided NC]\n",  mean(kw_lfp$kappa0)))
cat(sprintf("  p_ml mean    = %8.4f  [should be ≈ 0.5; samesex near-random]\n", mean(p_ml_lfp)))
 
cat("\nIncome sample:\n")
cat(sprintf("  mean(kappa)  = %8.4f\n", mean(kw_income$kappa)))
cat(sprintf("  mean(kappa1) = %8.4f\n", mean(kw_income$kappa1)))
cat(sprintf("  mean(kappa0) = %8.4f\n", mean(kw_income$kappa0)))
cat(sprintf("  p_ml mean    = %8.4f\n", mean(p_ml_income)))
 
cat("\n")
cat("Interpretation:\n")
cat("  mean(kappa), mean(kappa1), and mean(kappa0) are all close because all three\n")
cat("  identify the complier share in population under Abadie's kappa theorem.\n")
cat("  Therefore mean(kappa0) should NOT be expected to be close to zero here.\n")
cat("  The key empirical issue in this design is not a near-zero kappa0 denominator,\n")
cat("  but the translation/unit sensitivity of the unnormalized estimators.\n")
cat("  The samesex instrument is close to randomly assigned, so p(X) is close to 0.5.\n")
cat(paste(rep("=", 60), collapse = ""), "\n")
 
```
